title: TO My Lord Colrane, In Answer to his Complemental Verses sent me under the Name of CLEANOR by Anne Killigrew — Deep Poetry Lines
content:
LOng my dull Muse in heavy slumbers lay,
Indulging Sloth, and to soft Ease gave way,
Her Fill of Rest resolving to enjoy,
Or fancying little worthy he

title: The Discontent. by Anne Killigrew — Sad Poetry Lines
content:
I.
HEre take no Care, take here no Care, my Muse,
  Nor ought of Art or Labour use:
  But let thy Lines rude and unpolisht go,
Nor Equal be their Feet

title: Upon the saying that my VERSES were made by another. by Anne Killigrew — Sad Poetry Lines
content:
NExt Heaven my Vows to thee (O Sacred Muse! )
I offer'd up, nor didst thou them refuse.
  O Queen of Verse, said I, if thou'lt inspire,
And warm my So

title: ON THE Dutchess of Grafton Under the Name of Alinda. by Anne Killigrew — Sad Poetry Lines
content:
A SONG.

I.
TH'ambitious Eye that seeks alone,
Where Beauties Wonders most are shown;
Of all that bounteous Heaven displays,
Let him on bright Alinda

title: HERODIAS Daughter presenting to her Mother St. JOHN's Head in a Charger, also Painted by her self by Anne Killigrew — Love Poetry Lines
content:
BEhold, dear Mother, who was late our Fear,
Disarm'd and Harmless, I present you here;
The Tongue ty'd up, that made all Jury quake,
And which so ofte

title: St. John Baptist Painted by her self in the Wilderness, with Angels appearing to him, and with a Lamb by him by Anne Killigrew — Nature Poetry Lines
content:
THe Sun's my Fire, when it does shine,
The hollow Spring's my Cave of Wine,
The Rocks and Woods afford me Meat;
This Lamb and I on one Dish eat:

title: The Miseries of Man by Anne Killigrew — Sad Poetry Lines
content:
IN that so temperate Soil Arcadia nam'd,
For fertile Pasturage by Poets fam'd;
